disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe pounding headache that came on over the day and worsened in the evening
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me about the headache?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Their head is pounding — it built up all day at work and got much worse after they got home. It's the worst they've ever had.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Repeated vomiting; unable to keep down fluids or oral medication
trigger_question_examples: Can they keep any water down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They've been vomiting non-stop and can't keep any water or their tablet down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Extreme intolerance of light and sound
trigger_question_examples: Does light or noise make it worse?
Can they tolerate any light or sound?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Any light or noise makes it much worse — they flinch and cover their ears if you speak normally and they have the room pitch black.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no neck stiffness reported by the patient/roommate
trigger_question_examples: Do they feel hot or have a fever?
Is their neck stiff or sore when they bend it forward?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, they don't feel hot and their neck isn't stiff or sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The headache built up gradually over hours, not a sudden explosive onset
trigger_question_examples: Did the headache come on suddenly like a thunderclap, or build up gradually?
How quickly did it reach its worst?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It built up over the whole day and got worse this evening — it wasn't a sudden explosion of pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new weakness, no slurred speech, no confusion — patient is alert and oriented
trigger_question_examples: Any weakness or numbness on one side, drooping of the face, or trouble speaking?
Are they still fully alert and making sense?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No weakness or drooping, no slurred speech — they're alert, just in agony and answering with thumbs up and down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Attempted an oral migraine tablet two hours ago and vomited it straight back up
trigger_question_examples: Have they taken anything for it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They took one of their sumatriptan 50 mg tablets about two hours ago but threw it straight back up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent similar episodes previously, always triggered by bright lights and loud, busy environments
trigger_question_examples: Is there anything that usually sets these off?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, they get these when they're stuck in bright, noisy places — but it's never been this bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recently started at a new open-plan office and finds the fluorescent lighting and noise overwhelming; feels out of place in the environment
trigger_question_examples: What is it about the office that affects them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They just started a job in a big open-plan office with harsh lighting and constant noise, and they find it completely overwhelming — they feel like they don't belong in a space built for everyone else.
